title: Tremor and Bradykinesia - Parkinson Disease
chief_complaint: Progressive right hand tremor at rest for 1 year, now involving right leg. Movements have become slow, and handwriting has become small. Wife notes he blinks less and has a masked face.

physical_exam.neuro: Resting pill-rolling tremor right hand, bradykinesia, cogwheel rigidity right arm, shuffling gait, decreased arm swing, masked facies, micrographia

hidden_data.datscan: Reduced dopamine transporter uptake in bilateral putamina, right > left

key_learning_points: TRAP: Tremor, Rigidity, Akinesia, Postural instability

patient_responses.tremor: It's worse when I'm resting, gets better when I move
patient_responses.walking: I feel stiff and my steps are smaller
patient_responses.handwriting: My writing has gotten tiny, I can't control it

feedback.correct_message: Excellent! Parkinson disease with classic asymmetric features.
feedback.incorrect_message: This is Parkinson disease. The resting tremor + bradykinesia + rigidity are classic.

datscan.comment: Confirms but clinical diagnosis is sufficient.

feedback.attending_note: PD management: Carbidopa/Levodopa 25/100 TID starting dose. Titrate slowly. Physical therapy for gait and balance. Monitor for motor fluctuations and dyskinesias. Non-motor symptoms: depression, constipation, REM sleep disorder.
